delivery.raw: オンライン同期型+非同期型

summary:
To develop speaking and listening skills useful in communication with patients and other medical professionals.
The English needed for medical consultations and for dicussion of medical conditions, diagnosis,and treatment of patients.
Simulated doctor patient interviews. Specialist vocabulary.
Students should already have had English language training plus some medical experience.

plan.text:
The English needed for medical consultations and for dicussion of medical
conditions, diagnosis,and treatment of patients.
Simulated doctor patient interviews. Specialist vocabulary.
Students should already have had English language training plus some medical
experience.
年度によっては使用するテキストを変更することがあるので注意すること。
DOCTORAL CLASS ‑ MEDICAL ENGLISH
1 INTRODUCTION: GOOD COMMUNICATION IN A PATIENT INTERVIEW
2 CULTURAL AWARENESS
3 FIRST COMMUNICATION WITH PATIENTS IN A HOSPITAL
4 “ “
5 “ “
6 THE CHIEF COMPLAINT AND QUESTION TECHNIQUE FOR DOCTORS
7 “ “
8 CHIEF MEDICAL CONCERNS IN THE MODERN WORLD
9 TAKING A MEDICAL HISTORY / STRUCTURING A HISTORY AND A
PRESENTATION
10 “ “
11 “ “
12 TAKING A FAMILY HISTORY
13 “ “
14 “ “
15 FIRST TERM EXAMINATION
16 TAKING A SOCIAL HISTORY / OCCUPATIONAL HEALTH
17 “ “
18 “ “
19 LIFESTYLE AND ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH
20 “ “
21 “ “
22 TELEMEDICINE: NEW WAYS OF CONSULTING
23 “ “
24 EXAMINING A PATIENT / PATIENT AND DOCTOR LANGUAGE
25 “ “
26 “ “
27 GIVING RESULTS TO A PATIENT
28 “ “
29 REVIEW
30 FINAL EXAMINATION

textbooks:
Good Practice: Communication Skills in English
for the Medical Practitioner, by Ros Wright. CUP
prereq:
To develop speaking and listening skills useful in communication with patients and other medical professionals.
Also to increase familiarity with medical vocabulary and medical documents.
prep: 教科書・参考文献を利用した予習ならびに復習

officeHour.name: Ribble Daniel Brownson
officeHour.place: 2nd floor faculty office, General Classroom Building

extra.label: 成績評価に関する補足
extra.text:
Students must be able to practice role plays in English,and understand medical vocabulary.
Active participation in online class activities 25%
Online assignments 25%
Examinations 50%
